    Home › Ships › Symphony of the Seas

    Symphony of the Seas

    Royal Caribbean · Cruise · In service · built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique

    Symphony of the Seas carried the world's-largest title for four years and remains one of the highest-capacity cruise ships ever built.

    Length overall361.011 m
    Beam47 m
    Gross tonnage228,081 GT
    Passengers5,518
    Speed22 kn
    Delivered2018
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by ships.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Passengers per metre#9 of 19
    15.3 pax/m passengers (double occupancy) ÷ length overall
    Length-to-beam ratio#13 of 45
    7.68 length overall ÷ beam
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The Symphony of the Seas's length overall, against things you already know the size of.

    Symphony of the Seas361.011 m
    Eiffel Tower (height)330 m
    Titanic269.06 m
    Football pitch105 m
    Boeing 747-876.3 m
    Blue whale25 m
    You (probably)1.75 m

    The 747-8 bar is the longest airliner ever built — our sibling site aircraft.fyi measures everything that flies the same way.

    One Symphony of the Seas = 1.34 Titanics · 3.4 football pitches · 14 blue whales · 4.7 Boeing 747-8s laid end to end.

    #28 of 45 by length → #12 by gross tonnage → #5 by passengers → Run the Canal-Fit Checker → Sailing on her? Get cruise-ready →

    Specification

    The numbers

    Length overall361.011 m
    Beam (waterline)47 m
    Gross tonnage228,081 GT
    Passengers5,518 (double) / ≈6,680 (max)
    Crew≈2,200
    DeliveredMarch 2018
    Maiden voyageApril 2018, Barcelona

    Compare it

    Put the Symphony next to something

    vs Oasis →vs Wonder →
    vs Harmony vs Wonder vs Oasis Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousStar Princess Next →TI Europe

    Last verified: 2026-07-15 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    Symphony of the Seas spent four years as the largest cruise ship in the world, and did it the Oasis-class way: not by novelty but by refinement, stacking incremental gains on Harmony's design until she measured 228,081 gross tons.

    Like every Oasis-class ship she is really two towers of cabins with an open-air canyon between them — Central Park, with more than 20,000 live plants, running down the middle of the ship at deck eight. The class's genuinely strange achievement is making a vessel of this size feel like an outdoor resort rather than an enclosed hull.

    The title moved on to Wonder of the Seas in 2022 and then to the Icon class, but Symphony still sits comfortably inside the world's top ten by tonnage, and her seven-night Caribbean program from Miami is among the most heavily booked itineraries in cruising.

    The class

    The Symphony line

    Oasis class
    Fourth of six. Took the largest-ship title from sister Harmony in 2018; handed it to sister Wonder in 2022.
    Operators

    Who operates the Symphony

    All 1 operator of record, verified 2026-07-15. Figures marked ≈ are approximate.

    RCIRoyal CaribbeanCaribbean itineraries from Miami, with past Mediterranean seasons.1✓→
    Quick answers

    Symphony, asked and answered

    How long is the Symphony of the Seas?
    361.011 m (1,184 ft) length overall — the #28 longest of the 45 ships on ships.fyi, and 3.4 football pitches end to end.
    How big is the Symphony of the Seas compared to the Titanic?
    The Symphony of the Seas is 1.34× the Titanic's length — 361.011 m against her 269.06 m. By volume the gap is starker still: 228,081 GT against Titanic's 46,328 GRT — roughly 4.9 times the tonnage, measured across two different rule eras.
    Who operates the Symphony of the Seas?
    Royal Caribbean. Current status: in service.
    Does the Symphony of the Seas fit through the Panama Canal?
    Not the original locks — but yes through the 2016 Neopanamax locks: at 361.011 m by 47 m on a 9.32 m draft she clears the 366 × 51.25 × 15.2 m expanded gauge.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from operator, builder and registry sources and cited per row. Fleet and status figures are snapshots, marked ≈ where approximate and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. Ranks never mix units — cruise ships rank by GT, container ships by TEU, tankers by DWT. All silhouettes are original works of ships.fyi, drawn to true scale. Guides may contain clearly marked affiliate links; they never change what we recommend. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
